title: This Free Tool Does Everything Claude Cowork Does And Runs Locally #ai #artificialintelligence

eval.summary: A 60-second promotional short for 'Robote,' a local-first personal AI assistant that builds a knowledge graph from email and meeting notes, then uses that context for meeting prep, email drafting, document generation, and background agents. Claims to be a free alternative to Claude's planned collaboration features, runs locally, supports Ollama/local LLMs alongside Claude/OpenAI, and has MCP tool support, voice interaction, and web search. Zero technical depth — no repo link, no architecture detail, no benchmarks, no API documentation shown. The 'comment for link' mechanic is a follower-farming pattern, not a credible distribution signal. Core value proposition (persistent work context + proactive agents) overlaps heavily with Alan OS + CLAUDE.md + VIE pipeline. Multi-LLM support (Ollama, OpenAI, GPT) is anti-stack — Veritas is Anthropic-only by design. No evidence it passes P1 (API/export), P3 (token efficiency measurement), or P6 (auth). Cannot be evaluated without the actual repo.
eval.fit_rationale: The knowledge-graph-over-work-context capability is already served by Alan OS + CLAUDE.md + VIE; the multi-LLM architecture is incompatible with the Anthropic-only constraint.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: nothing — overlaps with Alan OS working memory (CLAUDE.md, session-log) and VIE intelligence pipeline without adding differentiated capability
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P7 immediately: the knowledge-graph-plus-proactive-agents pattern is the core design of Alan OS + VIE, meaning overlap is well above 60% with no BUILD_OR_EXTEND.md justification possible. Fails P3 because no token-efficiency measurement exists and no repo is available to evaluate. Fails stack constraints outright by treating Ollama and OpenAI as first-class peers, which contradicts the Anthropic-only LLM policy. The content itself is a 60-second follower-farming short with no technical substance to evaluate further.
